free_text: Experimental study of 53Cr via the (d,pgamma) reaction
#doi:10.1103/wp85-rthk
9-MV Super-FN Tandem Van de Graaff
accelerator at the John D. Fox Superconducting Linear
Accelerator Laboratory of Florida State University.
The Super-Enge Split-Pole Spectrograph
(SE-SPS) and CeBr3 Array (CeBrA) demonstrator, which
consisted of four 2x2 inch CeBr3 detectors and one
3x4 inch CeBr3 detector placed around the scattering
chamber.
A natural chromium foil of areal density rho = 300
microg/cm2 evaporated onto a natural 20-microg/cm2
carbon backing.
Particle-gamma coincidences between the SE-
SPS and CeBrA detectors. Two magnetic field settings
were used, 8.3 and 7.5 kG, to identify excited states
of 53Cr populated in the 52Cr(d,p) reaction up to
the neutron-separation energy, Sn=7937.12(14) keV,
with the position-sensitive focal plane detector of the
SE-SPS.
Stated uncertainties for the cross sections
are statistical uncertainties at the 1-sigma level,
if you want to add that. For states, where "average"
was given (unweighted average of the cross section
obtained at two different field settings) , the
uncertainty of that cross section corresponds to the
standard deviation determined from the two
cross-section values.
Approved by M.Spieker on March 9, 2026.
BP
Figs. 6-9
Data were provided by M.Spieker on March 2, 2026.
